Sam Craig, a New York sportswriter marries Tess Harding, a political columnist whose career comes first.
programme.title: Woman of the Year

programme.desc: A New York dancer (Fred Astaire) grooms a chorus girl (Judy Garland) to be his new partner, falling in love along the way.
programme.title: Easter Parade

programme.desc: A secret agent (Dean Martin) stops a Chinese villain's atomic plot in the first of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Silencers

programme.desc: A suave stranger (Cary Grant) helps a chic widow (Audrey Hepburn) stalked by four men looking for loot in Paris.
programme.title: Charade

programme.desc: Mark Twain's boy hero (Eddie Hodges) rafts the Mississippi with slave Jim (Archie Moore) and meets a bogus king (Tony Randall) and duke.
programme.title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn

programme.desc: A ballet dancer (Vivien Leigh) and a British officer (Robert Taylor) cross stars on the eve of World War I.
programme.title: Waterloo Bridge

programme.desc: Herman (Fred Gwynne), Lily (Yvonne De Carlo), Grandpa (Al Lewis) and their ghoulish TV family go to England to claim an estate.
programme.title: Munster, Go Home

programme.desc: Two con artists (James Mason, Gina Lollobrigida) bring an outlaw (Lee Van Cleef) into their scheme to bilk the Mexican government out of $1 million.
programme.title: Bad Man's River

programme.desc: Blondie (Penny Singleton) and Dagwood (Arthur Lake) join Mr. Dithers on a South American cruise.
programme.title: Blondie Goes Latin

programme.desc: A singing cowboy (Roy Rogers) clears the outlaw (Donald Barry) and his gang of a bank robbery done by bankers.
programme.title: Days of Jesse James

programme.desc: The Civil War Map Story Steve goes to Richmond to find out why a foreign agent has stolen an old Civil War map from the home of Col. Caruthers.
programme.title: Dangerous Assignment

programme.desc: S02 E12 — Hot Ice Cream A body goes missing from an amusement park.
programme.title: Man With a Camera

programme.desc: S01 E22 — Cupid's Question Column Blondie is asked to write an advice column for the local newspaper.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: S01 E19 — Lucy's Barbershop Quartet Lucy works to attend a barbershop-quartet competition.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: S02 E01 — Showdown A young bank robber gets a job on the Ponderosa to prevent the sheriff from finding the other members of his gang.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Batman (Adam West) and Robin (Burt Ward) save Gotham City from the Catwoman, Joker (Cesar Romero), Penguin and Riddler.
programme.title: Batman

programme.desc: Three bumbling janitors encounter a slew of curious creatures after accidentally launching a rocket to Venus.
programme.title: Have Rocket, Will Travel

programme.desc: A Broadway star (Bette Davis) takes a young and seemingly naive aspiring actress (Anne Baxter) under her wing.
programme.title: All About Eve

programme.desc: Two Wild West con men (John Astin, Steve Carlson) ride into a Colorado town, unaware it sits on a fortune in ore.
programme.title: The Brothers O'Toole

programme.desc: A bomber pilot (Spencer Tracy) goes to heaven and becomes guardian angel to another pilot (Van Johnson) courting his old girlfriend (Irene Dunne).
programme.title: A Guy Named Joe

programme.desc: A poor Chicago secretary (Diana Ross) takes the fast lane to fame as a model and a fashion designer.
programme.title: Mahogany

programme.desc: Astronauts (John Archer, Warner Anderson, Tom Powers) land their rocket on the moon but must lessen the load a lot in order to get back.
programme.title: Destination Moon

programme.desc: S02 E01 — Showdown A young bank robber gets a job on the Ponderosa to prevent the sheriff from finding the other members of his gang.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Two Navy inductees (Dean Martin, Jerry Lewis) go through basic training, then head for Honolulu by submarine for fun.
programme.title: Sailor Beware

programme.desc: Astronauts (John Archer, Warner Anderson, Tom Powers) land their rocket on the moon but must lessen the load a lot in order to get back.
programme.title: Destination Moon

programme.desc: The first manned flight to Mars is a flop, so a space official (Hal Holbrook) fakes it Hollywood-style for the public.
programme.title: Capricorn One

programme.desc: Flashbacks tell the story of a tenderfoot who rose to glory by gunning down the outlaw terrorizing his small town.
programme.title: The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir at Temple Square performs songs of hope, faith and inspiration; singing sacred hymns both new and old from around the world as well as uplifting selections from the American songbook.
programme.title: Music & the Spoken Word

programme.desc: A ballet dancer (Vivien Leigh) and a British officer (Robert Taylor) cross stars on the eve of World War I.
programme.title: Waterloo Bridge

programme.desc: Mark Twain's boy hero (Eddie Hodges) rafts the Mississippi with slave Jim (Archie Moore) and meets a bogus king (Tony Randall) and duke.
programme.title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn

programme.desc: A corporate climber (Jack Lemmon), whose boss (Fred MacMurray) and others use his apartment for hanky-panky, aids a young woman (Shirley MacLaine).
programme.title: The Apartment

programme.desc: A heroin addict is caught between two Times Square narcotics agents and the pusher they are trying to bust.
programme.title: Born to Win

programme.desc: A Broadway producer (Zero Mostel) and his accountant (Gene Wilder) back a sure-fire flop: "Springtime for Hitler."
programme.title: The Producers

programme.desc: Holmes (Basil Rathbone) and Watson (Nigel Bruce) follow a popular matchbook full of microfilm sought by Nazi spies.
programme.title: Sherlock Holmes in Washington

programme.desc: A rancher's (John Wayne) wife (Maureen O'Hara) summons him home to hunt down the outlaw (Richard Boone) and gang who kidnapped his grandson.
programme.title: Big Jake

programme.desc: The pirate (Robert Newton) and young Jim Hawkins (Kit Taylor) rescue Purity Pinker (Connie Gilchrist) and look for treasure.
programme.title: Long John Silver

programme.desc: When an outlaw (Lee Marvin) with a fake nose kills her father, a schoolmarm (Jane Fonda) hires his drunken twin to get revenge.
programme.title: Cat Ballou

programme.desc: A busboy is embraced by the beatnik art community when he develops an impressive but morbid method of sculpturing.
programme.title: A Bucket of Blood

programme.desc: A policeman's efforts to curb illegal hot rodders backfire when an auto crash claims the life of a racer's brother.
programme.title: Hot Rod Girl

programme.desc: When former business partners Dean and Jerry enlist in the Army, they have different ambitions. Martin wants to see action. Klutzy Lewis wants to stay out of combat. They both have to deal with stern Army personnel while preparing for a variety show.
programme.title: At War With the Army

programme.desc: An ex-convict (John Payne) is framed for a bank holdup done by a police captain (Preston Foster) and men wearing masks.
programme.title: Kansas City Confidential

programme.desc: The frontiersman (Bruce Bennett) leads settlers to Kentucky, where he makes peace with a Shawnee chief (Lon Chaney).
programme.title: Daniel Boone, Trail Blazer

programme.desc: A cattle baron (John Wayne) tries to tame his wife (Maureen O'Hara) amid a feud with settlers and trouble with Indians.
programme.title: McLintock!

programme.desc: During the Civil War, reports of a sea monster attacking ships alarm the Navy. An investigation finds the monster is a submarine, the "Nautilus," commanded by vengeance-seeking Capt. Nemo (Allen Holubar), who was once an Indian prince.
programme.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea

programme.desc: After returning to Hawaii from being in the army, a pineapple tycoon's rebellious son turns into an island tour guide with his girlfriend.
programme.title: Blue Hawaii

programme.desc: A reporter hears how an ex-convict (James Caan) and an Indian woman (Stefanie Powers) destroyed an Old West town overnight.
programme.title: Gone With the West

programme.desc: A secret agent blocks a villain's helio-beam plot in the second of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: Murderers' Row

programme.desc: While a Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor looms, an Army sergeant (Burt Lancaster), a former boxer (Montgomery Clift) and an officer's wife (Deborah Kerr) become entangled with others at an Army base on Oahu.
programme.title: From Here to Eternity

programme.desc: A chorus girl (Vera-Ellen) stars in a broke producer's (Cesar Romero) show after rumors link her with a millionaire (David Niven) in Scotland.
programme.title: Happy Go Lovely

programme.desc: Oscar-winning adaptation of the Broadway hit about an English professor (Rex Harrison) who teaches a cockney merchant (Audrey Hepburn) to be a lady.
programme.title: My Fair Lady
